jiub_greeting.speakerText: Ask what you came here to ask. I've walked farther than most and remembered more than I meant to.

node_stats_1.speakerText: Natural 20 adds six ability scores to your character: Strength, Dexterity, Constitution, Intelligence, Wisdom, and Charisma. Each one is a number from 0 to 30, and the higher a score is, the more it does for you in both combat and conversation. Type /sheet at any time to open your character sheet and see where they stand.

node_stats_2.speakerText: Every score pulls double duty. In combat, each one powers something specific. Strength hits harder with both your swings and your crits. Dexterity raises your crit chance and movement speed. Constitution deepens your HP and stamina. Intelligence expands your mana pool and boosts your elemental damage. Wisdom regenerates your mana and lands your on-hit effects more reliably. Charisma strengthens your party buffs and greatly affects your success in dialogue. These same scores add to your d20 roll whenever a skill check calls for one.

node_stats_3.speakerText: You earn experience as you explore, fight, and complete quests, and enough of it earns you a level. Every level up gives you a single ability point to place into any score you choose, so your character grows in whatever directions you push them. The loot you find scales to your level along with the strength of your enemies as you climb, so the lowest strength loot you find keeps pace with what you're fighting.

node_affixes_1.speakerText: Affixes are random bonuses that roll onto gear when it's generated. A weapon might add fire damage to every hit, armor might reflect damage back at whoever strikes you, a tool might vein-mine an entire ore deposit in one swing. Because the rolls are random, two drops of the same item type can end up with wildly different kits, and hunting for the right rolls is a huge part of the mod's loot loop.

node_affixes_2.speakerText: Gear comes in five rarity tiers: Common, Uncommon, Rare, Epic, and Legendary. Higher tiers carry more affixes at once, and each individual affix rolls in a higher value range. An Epic piece will almost always outperform an Uncommon piece of the same type, but exactly what it does for you is still a product of the rolls.

node_affixes_3.speakerText: Affixes grow stronger with your ability scores, the same way native Hytale stats scale off them. A weapon's fire damage grows with Intelligence, Resilience on your chestplate grows with Constitution, and most affixes are tied to one of the six scores this way. A few affixes also add points to a stat directly, which then feeds every other affix on your gear that is affected by that stat. Investing into a stat is investing into every affix it touches.

node_affixes_4.speakerText: Affixes fall into a few broad groups. Offensive ones add damage, crit chance, or life leech. Defensive ones absorb damage, let you dodge attacks, or turn thorns on whoever hits you. Utility ones do everything from faster mining to longer breath underwater. Weapons can add elemental damage or apply an elemental Weakness to a target, and armor can carry elemental Resistance to soften incoming hits of that element.

node_quests_1.speakerText: Settlers in towns and villages will offer quests when you talk to them. Accept one and it joins your Quest Log on your character sheet, where it stays until you finish it. Type /sheet at any time to open the log and check what you're working on.

node_quests_2.speakerText: Quests may unfold across multiple phases, each with its own objective and its own reward. When you finish an objective, you bring it back to the quest-giver to turn it in, and you collect that phase's experience and a rolled item reward immediately. Quest Objectives come in a handful of types: clear a count of enemies, gather a resource, fetch a specific item, talk to another NPC, or defeat a named boss. Kill credit is generous, any mob you've damaged in the seconds before it dies counts as yours.

node_quests_3.speakerText: Every NPC keeps a personal measure of you called Disposition, shown on their dialogue screen and moved by your actions with them. It increases when you accept their quests, finish objectives for them, and pass skill checks in their conversations. It decreases when you fail checks or refuse what they offer. Higher disposition with an NPC unlocks better rewards and dialogue paths from them specifically, lower disposition closes those same paths off.

node_quests_4.speakerText: Some dialogue options are marked with a skill in brackets, like [Persuasion] or [Intimidation], and choosing one rolls a d20 die against the skill check's difficulty. Your total is the die plus the modifier of the relevant ability score, plus a small bonus if you're trained in that skill. Disposition with that NPC affects the roll itself: high enough and you roll with advantage, low enough and you roll with disadvantage. A Natural 20 passes regardless of the math, and a natural one fails the same way.

node_parties_1.speakerText: Type /sheet any time to send a party invite, or accept one another player has sent you. When any member of a party accepts a quest, everyone in the party at that moment joins the quest with them. Quest rewards only pay out to members who were online and nearby when the objective completes, though gathering phases pay anyone online regardless of where they are. Each eligible member receives their own experience and a piece of loot rolled fresh for them, so no two people walk away with the same drop.

node_parties_2.speakerText: The world answers your numbers, and champion enemies spawning near a larger party rise in strength to meet the group facing them. Leadership transfers on its own if the leader has gone silent for a week, so a stale invite never strands an active group. The quests you agreed to together stay on the sheet of every member who was there at acceptance, whether your paths later diverge or hold.

node_parties_3.speakerText: Champion and boss mobs draw from the same affix pool you do. A champion with Life Leech heals off every hit it lands on you. A boss with Thorns damages you back every time you strike it.

node_mobs_1.speakerText: Natural 20 mobs never spawn alone. Every champion encounter is a group: one boss surrounded by three to seven champion minions, all sharing the same tier and the same biome. Champions in a group all carry the same set of affixes, and the boss carries their own separate set on top of a much larger health pool. You can tell a Nat20 mob from a native Hytale one at a glance, ours are always tinted with color and theirs are not.

node_mobs_2.speakerText: Every group rolls a single difficulty tier that colors the whole pack: Uncommon, Rare, Epic, and Legendary. Higher tiers hit harder, carry more affixes, and drop better loot, the bigger the fight and the bigger the payoff. Legendary is boss-only and rare, a group's champions cap at Epic even when their boss is gold.

node_mobs_3.speakerText: Natural 20 loot only drops from champion and boss mobs. Champions drop gear occasionally, while bosses drop reliably, always at least one piece and often several. You get credit for any mob you dealt damage to in the last thirty seconds of its life regardless of who struck last, so softening a boss and letting it fall to lava, another mob, or a partymate's finisher still counts as your kill.
